title: 10 best herbal creams for frozen shoulder

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with frozen shoulder because of their unique ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. Herbal teas like ginger, turmeric, and arnica are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that target the affected joint, easing pain and stiffness. The cream's topical application allows these active ingredients to penetrate deep into the skin, providing relief from the constant discomfort associated with frozen shoulder. As a result, tea creams offer an effective and natural way to alleviate symptoms, promoting recovery and improving overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas like ginger, turmeric, and willow bark contain compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in herbal teas such as green tea, ginkgo biloba, and rosehip help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by frozen shoulder, promoting healing and reducing scarring.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: Herbal teas like chamomile, lavender, and peppermint contain compounds that help relax muscles, reduce tension, and alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tea roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The bark of the tea plant contains flavonoids, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting blood circulation, both important factors in treating frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh camellia sinensis leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine them with 200ml of boiling water in a stainless steel pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to extract the active compounds.
Combine the extracted liquid with 50g of beeswax, 30g of coconut oil, and 20g of shea butter in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its unique blend of anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The herbs contained in the cream, such as ginger and turmeric, work together to reduce inflammation and ease stiffness in the affected area. Additionally, bell peppers are rich in antioxidants and vitamins that promote healthy joint function and reduce pain caused by frozen shoulder. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience relief from chronic pain and stiffness, improving their range of motion and overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Some herbal extracts like turmeric (Curcuma longa), ginger (Zingiber officinale), and willow bark (Salix alba) have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder.

Pain-relieving properties: Herbs like capsaicin (Capsicum annuum) from chili peppers, wintergreen (Gaultheria procumbens), and menthol (Mentha piperita) can provide topical analgesia, helping to numb the affected area and reduce pain.

Wound healing and tissue repair properties: Certain herbs like aloe vera (Aloe barbadensis), tea tree oil (Melaleuca alternifolia), and chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) may promote wound healing, reduce scarring, and enhance tissue regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The bark of the bell pepper plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to reduce stiffness and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and stems then wash them gently with distilled water.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or desiccant at room temperature for 7 days.
Grind dried capsicum annuum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle then sift through a mesh strainer to remove any lumps.
Mix 20 grams of ground capsicum annuum with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler then heat slowly until the mixture melts and reaches 160°f.
Remove the double boiler from heat let it cool slightly then whip the mixture using an electric mixer for 2-3 minutes to create a smooth creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with frozen shoulder because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient reduces inflammation in the affected joint, which is a major contributor to the stiffness and pain associated with frozen shoulder. The cream also contains flavonoids, which help to relax muscles and improve blood flow to the area. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, herbal licorice creams provide relief from the discomfort and stiffness of frozen shoulder, allowing for greater mobility and range of motion.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This property has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help relieve pain and discomfort in frozen shoulder patients.

Saponins: These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce oxidative stress associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, which may help alleviate stiffness and reduce joint pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 200 milliliters of distilled water and let it steep for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain a clear liquid extract, which will take approximately 10 minutes.
Combine the extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted, taking about 20 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 to 40 minutes before transferring to an air-tight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow creams helps with frozen shoulder because they contain salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that targets the source of pain and stiffness in the joint. As it penetrates deep into the affected area, salicin reduces inflammation, relaxes muscles, and increases blood flow, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort. By reducing swelling and heat in the joint, these creams also alleviate feelings of stiffness and rigidity, helping to restore flexibility and range of motion.
remedy_properties: Salicin content: Salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, helps reduce inflammation and pain in the affected joint, making it easier to move and alleviate stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area, allowing for greater mobility and range of motion.

Pain-relieving properties: The combination of salicin and other compounds in white willow bark extract helps to numb the pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it more manageable.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, the roots help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The barks contain salicylic acid, which acts as an anti-inflammatory and analgesic, making them effective in relieving the discomfort of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 50g of salix alba leaves, taking care not to contaminate or spoil the material during processing.
Grind the dried salix alba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, then sift through a 100-mesh sieve to ensure uniformity.
Combine the powdered salix alba with 20g of beeswax, 10g of coconut oil, and 5g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, stirring until fully incorporated.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the ingredients to melt and blend together, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Pour the warm mixture into sterilized glass jars and seal tightly, storing at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerating for longer shelf life.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for increased range of motion and reduced stiffness.
Antioxidant: Antioxidants in purple coneflower creams help protect the joint from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing inflammation.
Pain-relieving: Purple coneflower's analgesic properties can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like cinnamic acid and ferulic acid, which help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered echinacea with 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
Combine the mixture with 20 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil, heating until melted and blended, then let cool to around 60 degrees celsius.
Whip 100 milliliters of coconut cream until light and fluffy, adding the cooled echinacea mixture and blending until well combined and smooth.
Pour the creamy mixture into sterilized jars, sealing tightly and labeling with date and ingredient information, allowing to set at room temperature for at least two hours before use.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains Echinacea, a natural herb that reduces swelling and alleviates pain in the affected joints. It also has analgesic properties that help to numb the pain, making it easier to move and stretch without discomfort. By reducing inflammation and pain, purple coneflower creams provide relief from the stiffness and limited mobility associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for a faster and more comfortable recovery.

Mountain arnica creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which target the affected joint and surrounding tissues. The cream's active compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and pain, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility. Additionally, arnica's analgesic properties help alleviate discomfort, making it easier to perform daily activities and exercises that promote recovery. By reducing inflammation and pain, mountain arnica creams can provide significant relief for individuals suffering from frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Arnica's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder, allowing for increased range of motion and reduced stiffness.
Pain relief: The cream's analgesic and anti-pain compounds, such as helenin and sesquiterpenes, help alleviate the chronic pain and discomfort often experienced in frozen shoulder.

Wound healing and tissue repair: Arnica's stimulatory effects on blood flow and its ability to promote collagen synthesis can aid in the healing of damaged tissues and reduce the formation of scar tissue associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, root extracts have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.

Rhizomes: Containing a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, rhizome extracts have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate pain and reduce stiffness in frozen shoulder sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers and dry them properly for at least 7 days or 2 weeks in shade.
Grind the dried arnica montana flowers into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 grams of powdered arnica montana with 125 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for approximately 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches 160°f to 170°f.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then pour into jars or tubes and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The herbs used in these creams, such as arnica and capsaicin, also stimulate blood flow and relaxation, allowing for easier mobility and reduced stiffness. Additionally, stevia's natural analgesic properties help to numb the pain, making it an effective alternative to traditional treatments like cortisone injections or surgery. By using herbal stevia creams, individuals can alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms without harsh chemicals or invasive procedures.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal stevia creams contain compounds like flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant: Stevia's antioxidant properties can help protect the affected joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further tissue degeneration.

Analgesic: Some herbal stevia creams may contain analgesic compounds like capsaicin, which can help block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds that help alleviate stiffness and improve joint mobility, making it an excellent addition to topical creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stevia rebaudiana leaves and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Mix 50g of stevia leaves with 500ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 24 hours at room temperature and then strain it through cheesecloth.
Combine 200g of beeswax pellets, 100g of coconut oil, and the infused oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until melted and blended, then pour into sterilized jars and let cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which target the affected area to reduce pain and stiffness. The curcumin present in turmeric works to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, allowing for increased range of motion and reduced inflammation. Additionally, turmeric's analgesic properties provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder, making it a natural and effective remedy for alleviating symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected joint, easing pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of turmeric cream help protect the joint from further damage by neutralizing free radicals that can exacerbate the condition.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of turmeric cream help relieve pain and discomfort in the affected joint, allowing for increased mobility and reduced stiffness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Roots: Contain compounds that stimulate blood circulation and reduce stiffness, making them effective in relieving symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Collect 50g of dried turmeric root powder and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps.
Combine the sifted turmeric powder with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool to around 50°c before adding 5ml of sweet almond oil and 2g of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and seal them tightly, allowing them to set at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected joint. Additionally, comfrey's natural wound-healing abilities can help promote tissue repair and relaxation, which is especially beneficial for frozen shoulder sufferers who often experience limited mobility and stiffness. By applying comfrey cream topically, individuals may experience reduced discomfort and improved range of motion over time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Comfrey cream's anti-inflammatory compound, allantoin, helps reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which cause inflammation.
Analgesic property: Comfrey cream contains analgesic compounds like salicylic acid and flavonoids, which help relieve pain and numbness in the affected area, providing relief from chronic discomfort.

Wound-healing property: Comfrey cream's high concentration of allantoin and other glycosides accelerates wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and enhancing skin elasticity, which can aid in the recovery process of frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a key compound that helps to stimulate collagen production and promote wound healing, making it ideal for soothing and repairing damaged tissues associated with frozen shoulder.

Barks: Containing high amounts of salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and swelling caused by frozen shoulder, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and flowers on a sunny day ensuring optimal potency.
Dry the comfrey plant material using shade drying or air circulation for at least 24 hours.
Combine dried comfrey with carrier oils such as coconut oil and olive oil in a 1:5 ratio grinding into fine powder.
Mix the powdered herbal blend with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler heating until melted and smooth.
Remove from heat and stir in a small amount of distilled water allowing the cream to cool and thicken for approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. The anti-inflammatory properties in wormwood creams help to calm down the affected joint, reducing stiffness and pain associated with frozen shoulder. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenoids, help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area by inhibiting prostaglandins and leukotrienes.
Pain-relieving: Wormwood's volatile oils, including thujone and camphor, have analgesic and anesthetic properties that help numb pain and discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant: Wormwood's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the affected tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The barks of the wormwood plant contain glycosides, which help to reduce swelling and ease joint stiffness in cases of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20 grams of fresh artemisia absinthium leaves in a warm oven for 2 hours at 150°f.
Combine the dried artemisia absinthium leaves with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over medium heat.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain and discard the solids before adding 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated, then pour it into sterilized jars and store in a cool, dark place.
Allow the cream to mature for 2 weeks before using, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper mixing and consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with frozen shoulder because they contain a unique combination of anti-inflammatory compounds that work to reduce pain and stiffness. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, target the affected area, alleviating discomfort and promoting mobility. Additionally, highbush cranberries have natural analgesic properties that help reduce pain signals to the brain, providing fast relief from frozen shoulder symptoms. Immune system support: The flavonoids and other compounds found in highbush cranberries have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which could potentially aid in the treatment of inflammatory conditions like frozen shoulder by enhancing immune response and reducing inflammation.

Pain relief: Some studies suggest that certain compounds found in highbush cranberries, such as proanthocyanidins, may have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that could help alleviate pain associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the highbush cranberry are believed to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them an effective ingredient in creams for frozen shoulder.
Rhyzomes: With their ability to stimulate blood flow and reduce pain, rhyzomes are a popular choice for medicinal creams aimed at alleviating the discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including vaccinium macrocarpon powder 250mg, coconut oil 50ml, beeswax 25g, and vitamin e oil 10 drops.
Combine powdered vaccinium macrocarpon 250mg with melted coconut oil 30ml in a double boiler stirring until smooth preparation time 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and add beeswax 20g stirring until fully incorporated and cooled completely cooling time 30 minutes.
Add vitamin e oil 5 drops to the cooled mixture stirring well to combine and prepare for filling containers filling time 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store in a cool dark place shelf life 6 months or as indicated by expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce pain and stiffness in the affected joints. The soothing gel extracted from aloe vera plants also aids in reducing swelling and promoting relaxation of tight muscles, allowing for greater range of motion and reduced discomfort. Additionally, aloe's natural cooling effect can help ease tension and calm irritated tissues, providing relief from the constant ache associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe creams contain compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and cinnamic acid that reduce inflammation and pain in the affected joint, helping to alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves of about 10-12 inches long and free of blemishes.
Extract the gel from the inner part of the leaf using a spoon or a specialized tool.
Mix 20 grams of the extracted aloe vera gel with 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for about 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 120°f to 130°f.
Allow the cooled mixture to thicken completely before transferring it to a sterilized glass jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as californianum and sesquiterpene lactones, work to reduce inflammation and stiffness in the affected joint, allowing for increased mobility and reduced pain. Additionally, pot marigold's antispasmodic properties help relax muscles and tendons, further alleviating discomfort associated with frozen shoulder.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream help protect the damaged tissues in the shoulder joint from further damage, promoting healing and reducing scar tissue formation.

Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation of the muscles, easing symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of organic sunflower oil and steep for 8 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the infused oil and heat gently over low temperature stirring constantly until melted completely.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars filling to the top and allowing it to cool before capping tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The herbal extract has a natural ability to reduce swelling and ease stiffness in the affected joint, allowing for increased range of motion and reduced discomfort. Pain-relieving: The cream's analgesic properties, attributed to its high concentration of beta-damascenone, help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by frozen shoulder, making it easier for patients to move their joints and perform daily activities.

Flowers: Damask rose flowers contain a high concentration of salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the affected joint.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried rosa damascena flowers and sweet almond oil for a 10% extract ratio in a glass jar.
Steep 50g of dried rosa damascena flowers in 100ml of boiling distilled water for 15-20 minutes to create an herbal infusion.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup and discard solids within 30 seconds.
Mix the filtered herbal infusion with 200ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup and heat gently for 1-2 hours at 70-80 degrees celsius.
Allow the infused cream to cool completely, then transfer it to sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before use within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort creams helps with frozen shoulder because it has anti-inflammatory properties that target the affected area, reducing pain and stiffness. The cream's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to alleviate inflammation and promote healing, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in triterpenoids and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The barks contain hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving the symptoms of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20g of fresh st john's wort (hypericum perforatum) leaves and flowers to obtain the dried herb.
Grind 10g of dried st john's wort into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release its active compounds.
Mix 5g of beeswax, 5g of coconut oil, and 1g of vitamin e oil with 20g of distilled water in a heat-proof bowl to create the cream base.
Add the powdered st john's wort to the cream base and stir until well combined, allowing it to steep for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The lemongrass oil in these creams has been shown to reduce pain and stiffness in affected joints, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility. Pain-relieving property: The cream's analgesic properties help block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from chronic pain and stiffness often experienced with frozen shoulder.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning after dew has dried, yielding one pound of greenery.
Chop the harvested cymbopogon citratus leaves into small pieces and steep them in 500ml of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out as much juice as possible from the solids.
Mix the resulting herbal juice with 200g of coconut oil and 100g of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl, heating the mixture to 60°c for 30 minutes.
Pour the hot cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature over several hours before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with frozen shoulder because they provide a soothing and cooling relief to the affected area. The menthol in peppermint relaxes the muscles, reduces inflammation, and improves blood circulation, which can help reduce stiffness and pain. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint may help reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected joint. Analgesic properties: The menthol and other active compounds in peppermint creams have analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, which can help alleviate the numbness, stiffness, and pain caused by frozen shoulder.

Vasodilatory effects: Peppermint creams can help improve blood flow to the affected area by dilating blood vessels, which can aid in the healing process and reduce the stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots are effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce muscle spasms and relieve joint tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried peppermint leaves mentha x piperita and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered peppermint with 100 grams of petroleum jelly and 50 grams of coconut oil in a glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients are fully incorporated and warm to the touch.
Add 20 drops of lavender essential oil and stir well to combine before pouring the mixture into sterilized jars.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before applying it topically to affected areas 3 times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with frozen shoulder because they contain potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce joint pain and stiffness. The natural oils in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, work to relax tense muscles and increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing scar tissue formation. Additionally, fennel's warming effects help to dissolve adhesions and promote range of motion, making it easier to move the shoulder joint and alleviate the stiffness associated with frozen shoulder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can help reduce swelling and pain associated with frozen shoulder by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and anesthetic properties of fennel, particularly its ability to stimulate the release of dopamine, can help alleviate pain and stiffness caused by frozen shoulder, allowing for improved mobility and range of motion.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Fennel's antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds can help protect against oxidative stress and reduce the risk of infection or further tissue damage in the affected joint, promoting a faster recovery from frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and swelling associated with frozen shoulder.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain essential oils that help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension, providing relief from the stiffness and limited mobility of frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground foeniculum vulgare flowers with 200 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup, stirring occasionally over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 20 drops of lavender essential oil and 15 drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture, stirring well to combine, then remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible, discarding the solids after 10 minutes of straining.
Allow the cream to mature in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily, before transferring it to a sterilized jar and refrigerating until use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with frozen shoulder because it targets the underlying inflammation and pain associated with this condition. The anti-inflammatory properties of dandelion root, combined with its natural analgesic qualities, work together to reduce stiffness and discomfort in the affected joint. Additionally, the cream's ability to promote blood flow and relaxation can help alleviate tension in the surrounding muscles, further reducing the symptoms of frozen shoulder. By addressing the root causes of pain and inflammation, dandelion creams provide a natural and effective solution for managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, helping to alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder.

Pain-relieving properties: The presence of sesquiterpenes, a type of compound found in dandelion, has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, providing relief from chronic pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion cream's high antioxidant content helps protect the affected tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, promoting healing and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help reduce pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder.
Leaves: High in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as calcium and iron, dandelion leaves provide a soothing and nourishing topical treatment for frozen shoulder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried taraxacum officinale roots and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered taraxacum officinale with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at a low temperature of 60°c for 30 minutes to facilitate absorption and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with frozen shoulder because of its potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The herbal extract soothes and calms irritated joints, reducing stiffness and swelling that often accompanies this condition. Additionally, chamomile's natural antioxidant effects help to protect the joint from further damage and promote healthy tissue regeneration. By applying chamomile cream directly to the affected area, individuals can experience improved range of motion, reduced pain and discomfort, and enhanced overall joint function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, reduce swelling and pain by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenes have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety that can exacerbate frozen shoulder symptoms.
Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's constituents, including chamazulene, relax muscle spasms and reduce stiffness, which helps to ease pain and improve range of motion in frozen shoulder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, an antioxidant that helps alleviate stiffness and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them properly for future use.
Mix 20 grams of dried chamomile flowers with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for two weeks to allow for infusing and straining.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids after one hour.
Whip 200 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of the infused chamomile oil until it forms a smooth and creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for frozen shoulder are topical preparations that combine natural ingredients, such as arnica, ginger, and turmeric, to provide relief from pain, stiffness, and limited mobility associated with frozen shoulder. These creams work by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and relaxing muscles, allowing for greater range of motion and reduced discomfort. Examples of herbal creams that help with frozen shoulder include Arnica Gel and Turmeric Cream. By using these creams, individuals can experience improved flexibility, reduced pain, and restored normal functioning, significantly enhancing their quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frozen shoulder with herbal creams is by using a combination of ingredients such as arnica, calendula, and devil's claw. Arnica reduces inflammation, while calendula soothes the skin and promotes healing. Devil's claw helps alleviate pain and stiffness. Apply these creams topically 2-3 times a day to affected areas, gently massaging into the skin until fully absorbed. This holistic approach can provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms without harsh chemicals or side effects.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frozen shoulder that are treated with herbal creams are conditions characterized by inflammation, stiffness, and limited mobility. These include arthralgia (joint pain), tendinitis (inflammation of tendons), and bursitis (inflammation of fluid-filled sacs). Herbal creams containing anti-inflammatory agents like capsaicin, arnica, and ginger can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and stiffness, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.
